chunk_summary: Diet diversity counseling under climate stress focuses on maintaining energy, protein, and micronutrient density using locally feasible foods, while addressing food insecurity caused by climate events like floods or heat waves.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of diet diversity counseling in mitigating nutritional deficits during climate stress.
Identify locally available nutrient-dense food options to preserve diet quality under food insecurity.
Learn coping strategies to maintain adequate nutrition during climate-related disruptions.
Apply WHO and ICMR-NIN dietary guidelines to clinical counseling for vulnerable populations.
key_facts: WHO guidelines emphasize timely introduction of diverse complementary foods, adequate meal frequency, and continued breastfeeding.
Indian dietary guidance (ICMR-NIN, 2024) recommends minimizing HFSS foods and prioritizing minimally processed staples.
Millets and traditional grains can be used as diversification options when rice or wheat prices rise.
Counsel families to maintain one protein source daily and avoid diluting milk or skipping meals during food insecurity.
Fortified foods or supplements should only be used when clinically indicated.
Follow-up plans after climate events are essential to reassess growth and dietary diversity to prevent nutritional faltering.
reference_quote: The WHO complementary feeding guideline emphasizes timely introduction of diverse complementary foods, adequate meal frequency, responsive feeding, and continued breastfeeding where possible.
document_key_topics: Diet diversity counseling under climate stress
Coping strategies for climate-related food challenges
